title: calamagrostis varia subsp. montana
intro: Calamagrostis varia subsp. montana, also known as mountain fescue, is a type of grass native to mountainous regions of the United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of mountain fescue include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Mountain fescue can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
